reviews.review_text:
This game cured my craving for cards, my wallet thanks you, and scalpers should hate you.

1. For starters, the game is great. I played the entire vanilla experience to completion instead of using it as a plain pizza dough and suffocating it under mod toppings to crush super man, and I feel that is important to mention if I am to review the game for what it is. 

This game has a lovely level of simplicity to just start pick it up and playing and has so many fun things to do and learn and it starts to develop a crisp level of complexity and strategy as you level up and unlock more products and hire more employees and can start filling the tournament station with good rewards and get into your shop layout to not only be eye candy, but also strategic to make money, the game just all starts to move together so smoothly as you keep playing and I am so insanely addicted due to it, I am 320+ hours deep and I not even close to being done sinking my teeth into this Michelin star meal I've been treated to with all that's available in early access. 

2. Now I'm gonna start to get borderline ignorant towards how hard it is to make a video game in this next bit, which involves what I think could be the games potential.

Much more product in existing types, but also potentially new types of product. Me personally, I'm here to just make a card shop I really enjoy walking around, and I am getting a level of that, but the potential for new decoration, types of things to sell, hanging shelves, shelves that are dual display, and selling, furniture, TV's, lights, clothes, The Bellagio Fountain in Las Vegas in the middle of my store? vending machines?? maybe simulate some of that Pokémon vending machine violence happening in the world??? this game I think could be crammed with endless types of things to just keep growing. My personal want of just making a lovely store, all exp midmaxxing aside and money and all that, could have me sinking even more hours into this game. 

One or two more TCG's besides Tetramon would be awesome, Just like real card stores. 

More employee options, and more employee's.

3. Who wants to hear me complain?

After my time with the game there are some outliers in what hinders enjoying the game fully and makes me want to take a break from a session.

Object collision. I have used EVERY thing you can put in the shops from every shop tab in the goofiest ways imaginable with a means of having no collision with mods, and it all works well, and allows me to use things in a more realistic sense to what a register section, shelving area, depot, and card display looks like in actual card stores. When I switch off no collision it just goes back to being such a headache with placement. I just don't quite see why the card tables, pack machines, and register need more personal space than an ex girlfriend really looking to break up with me. Maybe sell a variant of the register with a card table attached to it or maybe just a whole double register if collision has issues that I am just not aware of? Wider shelves and displays with curvatures? I just really don't understand why some items need space that are bigger than the item itself. 

A dedicated storage room would be a game changer. Me and many players alike keep using shop B as our depot to store stock, and it really bothers me because I imagine shop B is supposed to be as a section for whatever it is we want, a play room, display room, anything. And seeing a bunch of supply shelves from the open front door is just such an eye sore and thinking to myself how it could be used for something really cool, and I am not gonna have my workers doing their thing while customers are bumping up next to them trying to play or purchase 13 of the same volume comic book, it just doesn't add up and It's sad that shop B is forced into being a supply room. 

Late game optimization is needing some love, when you get to a point where you have every type of product and max out all of the room levels and fill it all up with items, the framerate starts to get to 1 frame per sometimes and you can temporarily relieve it by relaunching the game for a certain amount of time, one of my shops is at a point where even if I relaunch the game is constantly trying to hold the world up anyways, which made me want to start a new shop to keep enjoying the game.

The glass in the game looks like 1 million deprived weirdos are breathing on my glass wondering what I'd do if they were in the shop alone with them. It's so foggy. Pretty please make the glass more clear?

Customers looking to trade a card more worthless than a half eaten disposable shot glass from a Paul Brother's boxing event gets real old real fast. Even worse when you deny their trade in half a second, they shake their head no and leave the store. "Dude didn't wanna buy my 3 graded poop from a butt? I really needed that 13 cents. :("
Walking around telling customers no becomes annoying when you're at a level when people start bringing in the big kahuna cards to trade. I'm not saying only have customers offer holy grails, just certain card types and values being ruled out as you level up would be nice. 

4. This game is insanely good, well worth your currency, and a whole lot of fun in ways you don't expect, for early access too. I want everyone to play this game and can't wait to see what's added. HVAC approved.

Great game, but Please rework the leveling....Lvl 100 is not fun, its not hard, its just a chore. Its not needed to get your level that high but it does unlock a larger area for your shop. Only 1% of players are getting to all of shop B unlocks. that is a problem

I bought this game thinking it’d be one of those “play for 2 hours and forget about it” simulator games. Somehow I looked up and had 30+ hours in it, reorganizing shelves at 2AM like my life depended on it and ripping open packs in the back room like a full blown addict.

This game has absolutely NO right being this addictive.

It starts simple: stock shelves, sell cards, make money. Then suddenly you’re optimizing shelf layouts, chasing rare pulls, and judging customers the second they walk through the door. Watching your tiny shop slowly turn into a legit card store is insanely satisfying.

The pack opening system is dangerous too. Whoever designed it knew exactly what they were doing. Pulling a rare card hits like a dopamine nuke and next thing you know you opened half your inventory instead of selling it.

What really surprised me is how authentic the game feels. It somehow captures the exact vibe of a real local card shop — the chaos, the excitement of big pulls, awkward customers, and the constant grind to keep everything stocked.

Huge respect to the devs because this feels like a game made by people who actually care. I kept saying “one more hour” and suddenly it was 4AM.

10/10 simulator.
Avoid if you value sleep.

Good game, but the updates are way too slow. 6 months into this year now, and we've gotten one minor update. Very bad for a game with early access, and I must say- not really been any major updates since 2024. This is game has sold more than enough for content to be released quicker than this. Sick and tired of this new "early access" model that promises so much when you purchase in how fast you'll get the complete game (usually 1 year), and fails to deliver every single time.
Not into TCG at all, but this is one hell of a fun and addicting game. One of the better simulator games I've played.

This game used to be a lot of fun, but now it is just plagued with bugs. I have people in my card shop that have materialized out of thin air with "stink clouds" even though I have multiple automatic machines to spray people in my store. Using the deodorant spray no longer works! I can't spray with a brand-new can anymore! I can't close the trade binders anymore either. The game is way too buggy now and it is just a frustrating mess.
Really fun and addictive! Slowly expanding my shop! The stinky customer mechanic can be annoying but its a great game.

Loved the game but the updates have broken it completely. No music, day time doesn't advance, game reverted back to "day 1" with no clock. Can't close the store and advance to the next day. I've basically completely lost my original save unless by some miracle it gets revived with another update (doubt it). I'm not spending another 20 hours to get back to where I was. Wait until they release a final version.

Really enjoyable game and very easy to get yourself lost in it. your like "ill just play for a little while and build my shop up" Next thing you know its been 5 hours and your changing your lay out to fit the decor and extra room for stock while making sure all the Smellys do not come in 10/10

An amazing game. Managing and growing your own TCG shop is a lot of fun, and collecting cards by opening packs is incredibly satisfying. Great mod support and frequent updates make it even better. Highly recommended!
great game love the simple gameplay loop
At first this looks  like the avarage sim game but its not its a realy polished and good sim game.
It's so addicting to play. I've played over 50 hours so far and I recommend it!
Games like this scratch that pokemon-nostalgia itch perfectly. Surprisingly addictive gameplay loop.
good chill game, lots to do, can be very repetitive. You can add mods easily lots of youtube tutorials to help you figure it out.
I have enjoyed this game very much, put several hours into it. Look forward to any future add-ons to keep me going

Really well made and really fun and addicting. Although there do exist some early access bugs, I would still highly recommend this game to anyone. Progression is very smooth and the overall vibe never feels overwhelming. Just calm and cruising through objectives day by day while expanding at a really fair pace.

Very nice and relaxing game, i like listening to DND horror stories while managing the store, it helps keeps my hands busy while keeping my head engaged, its not too complicated to figure out, you can run the store automatically with employees and machines, or you can do it all manually, very nice.
It's fun and relaxing, and has a light gamble element to it which is fun. Good game to watch a movie while playing.
The game is genuinely well made. I LOVE the gacha in it (pulling packs) and i like all the options and everything in it! Definetly recommend and caseoh has a good taste in recommending games.
Love - didn't even know it was in early access. However, looking at more of the early access items the game looks like it will be even better!

a game that i streamed that is fun
very fun to play. its like opening a pokemon card shop which is fun like pigni :pigni: i love this game please get it
it was fun to stream and is fun to do whatever you posibly want plz have fun and buy it, it is the best.
i do think it could add more items like the 30 pack boosters but thats for the future
nice relaxing game, definitely recommend if you like simulator games
One of my Favorite shop games on steam right now. Especially if you want to hit that Pokémon like pack opening, tournament's, and tons of different items for sale.
Great game overall the cards are so imaginitive and i just love to play around.

This has been a fun game. There has been a time or two where customers spawn in the location of future expansions. Which happened to me during a tournament and 3 of the people in it were trapped so it never got out of the first round.

Very good game, super entertaining and a lot of hours can go into it without you even realizing it, but it is just too unoptimized to be playable when you get to a certain point. Once your shop advances a bit and you start placing more cards / displays / products the fps just fully sink. I’m talking super choppy, 20/30fps max even with no customers in the shop.

I did not think that I would end up liking this game as much as I did.  kinda adicting in a way that just makes me want to play more and more.  not easy at all and there is some thinking involved 10/10 would recommend
This game is great if you are looking for a game that is not a nerve racking one. I would highly recommend this game.
A great cozy simulation game. So easy to get lost in it for hours though, lol, so be careful if you're someone like me who looses track of time. Highly recomend!
It is exactly as described. You lose track of time while playing, similar to many games of the genre.
I really enjoy this game, but it is better with the Pokemon mod. Overall, I give the base game a 4.2/5 because it is fun but gets kind of repetitive.
Only thing i would suggest is pushing it to the limits with characters. Be another pal world
i really enjoyed playing this game, and its probably the best tcg simulator ive tried so far

Really love this game, after about 90 hours I’ve unlocked everything but am still collecting cards. I’ve enjoyed all the updates so far and can’t wait for more to come. Only real complaint is I wish there was a multiplayer or co-op mode, as I’d love to play this game with friends.

If you collect cards, even though they may not be Pokemon-related or themed, you'll enjoy this game. You get to setup shelves, haggle to buy cards from customers, and spray stinkies with deodorant spray! Just like real collectors who can't bathe to save their lives!

Highly addicting fun in the vanilla game itself, you can open packs hire workers and restock you name it it is very fun. You ALSO can mod it and put in pokemon or yu-gi-oh cards instead of the tetramon or whatever lol. They also have some others like digimon. Anyways if you like simulation games and love the thrill of maybe today will be a big sale day or this pack is gonna have the 10k dollar card in it I know it then this game is for you!!

Fantastic game! Don't bother with any other similar titles, this is the only one you'll ever need. If you like ripping packs and hunting for chase cards with a side of cozy low-stakes shop management (all with ABSOLUTELY NO bugs or crashes!), this is the game for you. JUST REMEMBER TO PAY YOUR BILLS!!!

Only a few criticisms:
1. The early game employees feel like a scam. I wouldn't bother hiring either of the first two offered; they work waaaaaay too slow.
2. I know card trading is relatively new, but I wish there was a way to set a minimum value of cards you're looking to buy/trade. I get some people like filling out the collection, but when you're just trying to make money, getting offered a $0.06 card is a waste of time.
3. The English isn't perfect, but that doesn't take away from the game at all! Just keep in mind some descriptions may not be entirely grammatical.

I will update my review as the game progresses. I look forward to seeing what Ding Shen has for us next!

This game is honestly the perfect copium for TCG lovers 😂 It's fun especially when I’m craving opening card packs IRL but can’t buy any because of scalpers or high prices lol.

Moreover, the game is basically unlimited pack ripping as long as you can manage your shop, pay the bills, and keep your stocks up. It's fun game for anyone who loves trading cards and collecting rare pulls.

The game is relaxing and really addictive, but it lacks of some multiplayer (don't want to get a concurrent game, I love this one too much)  and small variants like having a real concurrent in the game. A bank system could be cool, even if I didn't need it personally, I saw some videos of people struggling with that, a "black label" equivalent could be cool too and some environment little stuff could be nice, can't wait to see the random event update.
Super chill game along the lines of Supermarket Simulator, but with the added fun of crackin' them decks.

I really need y'all to add multiplayer. I don't think you understand the AMOUNT of TISM I have for this game and my friend would not be able to contain the retizzler if you added multiplayer. I NEEEEEEEEEDDDDD IIIIIITTTTT!!!!!

But for real this game is great and I do really want to be able to play this with others. It would be so vibey to be able to run a tournament while that one friend is just pulling packs and the smelly Reddit mods are walking through the store getting hosed down.
great game for the casual shop simulater and some pretty cool cards to collect as well
really addictive game, I wasnt able to stop until I add all the achivement. I found it fun and relaxing.
this game is really fun and it feeds my gambling addiction with only spending 9 dollars and not 1000 dollars for actual pokemon packs. 5 stars
i had such a fun time ripping packs and not sending my family into generational debt. 10/10 game would recomend to anyone with a gambling addiction.
Addictive and allows for the experience of ripping packs and pulling good cards, but without going into crippling debt! More so than we already are!

Played the Chibi version on Mobile before. I love the collecting cards part. Saw this here as a 3d first person pov. Its quite relaxing. Early game is where you need more planning on how you progress on the game. Late game is like an idle. The rng for higher card value is a bit challenging. Im not lucky so its hard to get those that I need for the achievements. Hahaha. 9/10.

I love this game! I played it when it was initially released, and it has already come such a long way in the past year. The developer should be really proud! This game became 50% more fun, and it was already fun to begin with, which shows the developer is really listening to community feedback and taking suggestions.

Gameplay stuff: It's a little different than other shop simulators in the sense that things feel more expensive, especially initially. You might almost wonder if you are doing something wrong, but just keep going! Once you get to the mid game point, things really start to take off and it's very rewarding. The pace feels good overall, too. With all the updates, there is always stuff to do, and it changes as your shop evolves. 

Definitely recommend. :)
A calm stratagey game with mutiple tactics if you love collecting cards or time managment games id highly reccomend
Its a tcg shop simulator, just play it with mods for the tcgs you like and it goes from a 7 to an 8.5

pretty cool and fun relaxing game, I play it whenever I dont want to be pissed off at a video game for getting ♥♥♥♥ on by some 12 year old, so its fun and chill, I really like opening the packs and getting good and expensive cards

For the amount of fun hours  this game provides, it's worth the price and more. 
There is very few  little bugs here and there, but nothing gamebreaking and easy avoidable.

It is updated on regular basis from the single developer and that is also a plus. 
Overall, 8/10 is my vote.

Update: I'm changing my rating from negative to positive, with a few caveats.

I like this game and will probably continue to play it, even though I wish SteamDeck support was much more addressed. The rotation rate of the camera while playing on controller, that value should be exposed in the settings. The current rate at which the player turns on controller is almost unbearably slow.

The Card Catalogue is broken, it looks like you can place cards into it, and you can to a certain point but then once you get about ~1800 cards in there it seems like it cant hold more than that?

Overall its a good "sleepytime" "zone out" game and just do your tasks, play how you think you should play and enjoy the ride. Selling single cards seems to be pretty profitable, and manipulating the market to your advantage with the tourneys you run.

----
I've not been able to reproduce this bug since the first two times it happened to me:
THERE IS A MAJOR BUG ON STEAMDECK
- if you use a save slot to save your current playthrough, then the next time you load from that save slot the game bugs out, people are intersecting walls and lots of things appear broken. Most importantly the UI gets totally bugged out so you can't do anything and have to close the game entirely, and you can only play from your auto save slot.

Maybe give this game a chance but on PC only.

Very fun game, but recently I have been unable to grab packs off of the shelf, I know it is right click on the mouse, but I have tryed everything there isnt even the option for pack grabbing on the keybind menu anymore. Please fix this bug.
A very very solid simulator game, I buy this when many streamers play it ( A lil bit FOMO but yeah ). great game especially if you into those TCG things.

A lot of fun! I hate how the clerk's running the registers will just straight up leave the store at 9:00pm even if people still need to be checked out, but its not a game breaker. Can be a little grindy but it is overall really relaxing!

It's Fun! So far ive got to the 3rd set of Booster Packs, and even just opening them is super fun. Feels way better than supermarket simulator, tons more ways to do prices, add stock etc. Making money is kinda a joke? you can find cards in a £0.20 pack that is worth like £900+ lol and on one of my first i ended up with a pack higher in worth than the entirety of the store and it got sold for more too, so aslong as you arent a moron with cash, youll NEVER run out of money. Great game!
My only complaint is when you send cards off for grading it doesn't show the damage on the card until after  it returns from grading. Other than that. Amazing Game!!
It's fun and can sctratch an itch when you've got a hankering for this type of game. It's comfy to play and have a show to watch.

4.8/5 stars

I found this game after playing another cards shop simulator game because everyone online was talking about it. The devs are still adding features and there is a roadmap on the main menu, but no timeline.

You can get cards graded, but it's hit or miss on the quality. I have tried several times to get better graded cards by re submitting them, but most of the time they stay the same. I know it can be tempting to sell cards, but you'll make a lot more money by not doing so. The tables will also hinder your profits so don't waste your money on them until you unlock the ability to host tournaments. The more expensive tables serve no purpose other then aesthetics. As for the entry fee, it auto fills the amount needed to break even. I found that I can make the entry fee a little under the prize value for the fourth through eighth runner ups.

There's people that don't know what a shower is in this game and you will need to spray them to fix it. You eventually unlock an automatic sprayer to help with this but a few will still get through unless you only allow them to enter at one door. And set up two of them. I use the mid tier one that takes two sprays and the only time a stinker gets through is when it's a tournament. If this was a toggle, it would be great because they add no depth or strategy to the game. If you play with mods, there's a few that fix this very issue.

Money can be a little bit of a pain in the beginning because you unlock stuff before you can afford the licence, much less the merchandise so be patient. Customer will still come in and look for it, but you will be fine. everything has a value that shifts as time goes on. I don't pay attention to it and have my staff adjust everything to be 10% above value and rounded. All of my reviews say how cheap everything is but when I did 15%, my sales plummeted.

Overall, this is a fun game and worth the money if you can find it on sale.

this game is amazing and you can tell the devs care.
also no ai slop art. very based

I love this game but please help I've been so entranced and I miss my family.
10/10 I should touch grass.
Awesome game for early access, the second i bought it i became addicted. Highly recommend!

I personally love simulation games and this one is  fantastic. This game has great artwork on the tetramon cards. The gameplay is addicting trying to collect the cards while also building a profit heavy store I highly recommend this game to anyone that wants a quality simulation game that you can play for hours on end.

For how much is going off on screen it runs really well and my gaming rig is extremely quiet while playing this. It's the most engaging at the beginning and before you are almost fully automated with employees. At current end game i set the day off and afk coming back to restock the storage shelves at days end.

Very fun. i have enjoyed seeing the progression of making the shop basically run itself. This is my first "simulator" game and have recommended it to others. Purchased when on sale but it is well worth the full price. Rarely have I came across bugs, but its nothing a reload can fix. I look forward to future updates.

I really enjoyed this sim game. It runs really well and I enjoy the tournament update. It's very chill and just takes time to generate money through sales. Opening card packs was fun and there was a rush everytime I got a "rare" card. I'd highly suggest checking the guides for the game, especially the about shelves and tournaments. I never got a card worth $100,000, but I'll keep the game installed and play occasionally. I really wish the shop could be bigger, I always needed more space. So I wasn't able to decorate with the statues, but they look nice.
chill,me as a pokemon lover cures my gambling adictiion of opening packs.very fun
Fun game with lots of modding potential to keep things interesting. 100% would recommend if you're into sim type of games!

I enjoyed the simple approach to a TCG store as a simulator. The game does get very grindy until you hit certain milestones then the game sort of loses the "what's next" feeling that draws you in. 

Some drawbacks are the AI could use a bit more what ifs so that they feel like they are doing things correctly. The lag from the tournaments can make your game stutter. Collision is odd and can send you forward very far if your not paying attention to what your bumping into or standing on. 

Things I want to see enter the game are a "scryglass" app for the phone so that I don't have to remove cards from the tournament center to see their prices. Maybe integrate this feature with the market app so that you can see recent price trends on your retail items as well.

The next item I would like would be making larger "window" sized posters for deco.

Finally, a toggle when pricing items to manually to set the price to what you have set and not have an employee change it back to what it has been told. An example would be changing a price lower than what my workers are set to fire sale an item to clear it out and make room for a new item. I run my shop at market, minus ten percent, then round. Sometimes, I want to drop my slow movers an extra five percent while not hitting anything else.

Very addicting, but endgame is so buggy that it becomes almost unplayable, stuttering every couple seconds. The 500 Gem grades achievement has to be one of the worst achievements I've ever seen in a game, especially with how graded cards can't be organized very well. I have 100 hours with that as the only achievement I don't have, and if I continued to commit to it, it would take another 100 hours and would restrict myself to a playstyle that is just simply not fun: going afk until each day is over, repeated 200 times because there's nothing else to do that far into the game.

6/10

This was the first game in a long time that I woke up early to get back into. I had a week off during the Christmas break and lived in the card shop every free moment I had. You really feel like you opened and operate a card shop.

I needed something to relax with, something simple with some mild engagement. This scratched that itch. As an early access it is worth the bite, and if it gets better, awesome. I am about 50 hours in, my store is employed and almost runs itself while I manage inventory and keep the card machines rippin'.
good game but was selling a 7000$ card but the person grabed it last second when i started the next day and the card dissapered and i got no money

It's fun now, It will be more fun once its fully released.
minor issues, nothing mods cant fix.
worth a play if you enjoy cards, management and organization.

Pretty good game, fun and it tickles my OCD brain.
Just a heads up, sometimes people are naked. Don't know what happened to my version but bald naked dudes everywhere.

At 4.5 hours I'm HOOKED! Way more fun than I could imagine. And for 12 bucks too!

Game is still in early access and could use some updates but its still a darn good game.

This dude is giving UBI and EA a run for their money even though that's not hard these days. 10/10 would recommend.

Its really fun. It's just as fun as the video makes it look, and i think that everyone should have this game. one more thing, i love the way everything looks, the graphics are great!!!! totally recommend

outstanding game
*please add making (your preset options) bulk boxes to *some* of the workers task abilities

The games just another supermarket simulator game. its just like all the others, just with a little bit of a different interactive mechanic. Overall the games ok but kinda makes me sad with how lifeless the game feels. Ill give it a thumbs up but i really wish there was a 50/50 button.

Overall a very fun game so far. Road map looks good. I think otherwise the biggest ask I have is 2-4 person co-op. When the card game becomes playable it could make for some fun competition between friends. It honestly feels like a missed opportunity to not have it in the long run.
Fun but repetitive after a while, it is definitely a game you come back to after a while. As well as adding some mods for fun, otherwise the game is really good
It is a fun game with plenty of fun in store and tons of potential to build a even more addictive product over time as the dev is constantly updating and changing the overall experience.

Amazingly fun and addictive.  Two comfort changest I would like to see. 1. The more advansed pack openers should not get slower, they should just hold more cards. 2. The bulk card bundle table should not be a guessing game.  Change it so you select the bulk pack you want to make and then it tells you what variety of cards you need to give up to make the pack.  Leave card count restrictions in place.

I love this game! Its a lot of fun to mindlessly play for hours. The initial grind can be rough, but once you can afford employees to do the small stuff, its fun to rip packs and try to build your personal collection, while still sending expensive cards to grade for selling. The different ways you can build and decorate your shop are endless. You can utilize many different types of shelves in many ways to best suite your customers and employees. I really enjoyed building and changing my shop as ive leveled up. The ability to display cool rare cards around the shop, as well as using them for decorations big and small help add a nice touch to the store!
Game is so much fun. So much to do and new updates and new game addons all the time. You see something new almost everyday in the game!
chill game that developers are still working on and improving.

Hired employee so I can spend more time opening packs. Line builds up, employee is now working through the (not unlocked) warehouse. 15 Days in, the pile of Stock grows in the corner.

Really wish for the upgrades (furniture, physical space etc) that it told you what it does, what it's for etc

I've played this game so much and it's so addictive. It's similar to games like Supermarket Sim but with the added fun of TCG mechanics like opening cards, trading and grading. Every time I play I can't stop playing!
this game just keeps on getting better, great job devs. hope to see many more additions to the game. keep up the great work!

10/10 for the first 10 hours of playing.
5/10 after playing for 15 hours total.
2.5/10 after playing for 19 hours.
1.9/10 after playing for 24 hours.


Great game if you want 10 hours of gameplay with no getting bored. 
horrible if you expect 20 hours.

i recommend only buying on sale or if you have spare money.


Final Rating:
5/10

I spent the weekend playing this game, a little over 23 hours. I do not understand where the good reviews come from. It gets really repetitive, really fast. Like most people, I played Pokemon growing up, even Pokemon TCG Pocket when it came out in late 2024 for over a year. That was fun. This game, however, gets utterly boring quickly. After the 3rd hour, I wanted to refund it but you obviously can't refund games after 2 hours. I felt my life dying away.

Compare this game to a game like Slay The Spire where I have nearly 400 hours. Both games have repetitive elements in them, but Slay The Spire also has random elements in it to where each play is fresh and different from the last.

In this game, everything becomes a chore EXCEPT checking people out. But restocking shelves (which the NPC workers might not do it the way/order you like) or creating 100-card packs or having to answer customers constantly looking to trade or sell their cards, it all gets too laborious or dull quickly.

My luck with Early Access Games have been terrible.

very unoptimized, turns my gpu into a space heater, all settings crank gpu to the same levels so just play it at max. 
getting bad graded cards is too low of a % for grades 1-3 and are is random moving and doing anything with a unsleeved card has no effect on anything, grinding the last 2 achievements is a time suck still working on them ugh atp i just play something else while the day plays out and just send cards to get graded every 2 days

there is a lot of stuff to unlock but nothing about the gameplay loop ever changes or is added

i had fun but there is a lot of dead time while playing

The number one thing that i dislike about this game is how when we want to sell individual cards, they are displayed on a stand made for smartphones (lol). Why can't we have a dedicated selling binder, a binder where you put all cards to be sold there? like in real life? the customer comes in, take a look in the binder, picks whatever he likes and buys them. i get that this game was not made from zero, (its a copy-paste of supermarket simulator) but still, at least make things a bit more realistic. ill still leave a positive review because i believe there's still time to fix things. (and because theres no other direct competition with this game atm)
Started a card shop to make money. Ended up opening all my own packs and bankrupting myself for shiny paper.

I cannot recommand this game for now. The game is amazing don't get me wrong, this game is literraly an addiction, BUT, if you are like me and you like modding the game (which is for this type of game the point of it, adding new pack, card set etc) this game start to becoming less and less enjoyable if you mod your game and you start to have a lot of new expansions just because the game is poorly optimized. 

If you have played the game vanilla way, i suppose it should be fine but if you are interessed in modding just wait before buying it.
Overall too much grinding for me, its fun to look for cool cards but when I'm installing mods to speed up pack opening and therefore play the game less, it starts to feel like a waste of time
i love ripping packs but scalpers this isnt the exact same but still nice also you run a shop
